channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Tehran targets Bahrain and Kuwait after US strikes
item.description: The regional crossfire raised the risks that an interim agreement to halt fighting in the war could break down, putting the Middle East again at risk of a wider conflict.
item.content:encoded:
The regional crossfire raised the risks that an interim agreement to halt fighting in the war could break down, putting the Middle East again at risk of a wider conflict.

(Image credit: Hadi Mizban)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What happened in the opening day of the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey
item.description: NATO arrived in Ankara with a message of unity, but President Trump's renewed attacks on European allies have once again laid bare the tensions challenging the alliance's future.
item.content:encoded: NATO arrived in Ankara with a message of unity, but President Trump's renewed attacks on European allies have once again laid bare the tensions challenging the alliance's future.
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: What's at stake at the NATO summit in Turkey
item.description: NATO leaders hope President Trump's criticism of the alliance is aimed at getting Europe to spend more on defense. But some analysts fear Trump may have an ulterior motive.
item.content:encoded: NATO leaders hope President Trump's criticism of the alliance is aimed at getting Europe to spend more on defense. But some analysts fear Trump may have an ulterior motive.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: IOC moves to allow more Russian athletes back into the Olympic games
item.description: The IOC is moving to clear the way for Russian athletes to compete in Olympic events. The decision to ease sanctions comes as Moscow continues missile and drone strikes killing civilians in Ukraine.
item.content:encoded: The IOC is moving to clear the way for Russian athletes to compete in Olympic events. The decision to ease sanctions comes as Moscow continues missile and drone strikes killing civilians in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: International Olympic Committee lifts Russia suspension ahead of 2028 LA Games
item.description: The International Olympic Committee advised sports bodies to end a three-year program vetting Russians for neutral status ahead of qualifying events for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.
item.content:encoded:
The International Olympic Committee advised sports bodies to end a three-year program vetting Russians for neutral status ahead of qualifying events for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.

(Image credit: Cyril Zingaro)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Woman accuses Graham Platner of sexual assault. And, NATO summit begins
item.description: Graham Platner is facing calls to end his bid for U.S. Senate in Maine after a woman accused him of sexual assault. And, Trump made his dissatisfaction with NATO clear ahead of its summit this week.
item.content:encoded:
Graham Platner is facing calls to end his bid for U.S. Senate in Maine after a woman accused him of sexual assault. And, Trump made his dissatisfaction with NATO clear ahead of its summit this week.

(Image credit: CJ Gunther)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Ukraine to press NATO for anti-ballistic air defense after latest Russian attacks
item.description: Ukraine pushes allies in NATO for anti-ballistic air defense after the latest major Russian attacks kill more than 20 in Kyiv and surrounding area.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine pushes allies in NATO for anti-ballistic air defense after the latest major Russian attacks kill more than 20 in Kyiv and surrounding area.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis

item.title: Trump's NATO pressure campaign continues as summit begins
item.description: President Trump's arrival in Ankara kicks off another potentially tense meeting for NATO.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's arrival in Ankara kicks off another potentially tense meeting for NATO.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Explosions rock Damascus, wounding 18, as French President Macron visits Syria
item.description: The French president's office said that Macron was safe and that his meeting with Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa would continue as scheduled.
item.content:encoded:
The French president's office said that Macron was safe and that his meeting with Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa would continue as scheduled.

(Image credit: Ghaith Alsayed)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: NATO summit set to begin in Turkey
item.description: Ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, the illusion of appeasing Trump has vanished—and Europe is being forced to take primary responsibility for its own defense.
item.content:encoded: Ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, the illusion of appeasing Trump has vanished—and Europe is being forced to take primary responsibility for its own defense.
item.dc:creator: Teri Schultz
